title: Thelypteris tamandarei
taxonomy: Thelypteris tamandarei belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This species is a type of fern, and more specifically, it is classified under the order Polypodiales. At the class level, it is classified as Polypodiopsida and at the phylum level as Pteridophyta. The order Polypodiales is further divided into several suborders, with Thelypteridales being one of them. Thelypteris tamandarei is also known to be a part of the genus Thelypteris.

intro: Thelypteris tamandarei is a species of fern, specifically a type of deciduous fern belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Brazil, for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, wounds, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris tamandarei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as a tincture, to leverage its bioactive constituents for therapeutic purposes.
